---
title: "MP29 hockeystick candidate"
---

{{< include ../_includes/sprfmo-banner.qmd >}}

<div class="paper-title">MP29: All-index Hockeystick Candidate</div>

::: {.status-note}
**Status:** advanced candidate in the current `jmMSE` analysis. MP29 has not
been adopted. This page reflects the exact `jmMSE` version
`94d4d325ba46220f56097b3de90824c83b7cf3cd` and the 25 July 2026 presentation.
:::

## Identity

| Component | Current definition |
|---|---|
| Name in the `jmMSE` analysis | `tun29`, labelled MP29 |
| Stock/index indicator | `cpues.ind` |
| Indices | `Chile_AcousN`, `Chile_CPUE`, `Peru_Artis`, `Peru_Ind`, `Offshore_CPUE` |
| Reference years | 2019-2023 |
| Recent-data window | 3 years |
| How the indices are combined | Mean (`combine = "mean"`, `metric = "mean"`) |
| Harvest control rule | Hockeystick (`hockeystick.hcr`); multi-stock testing uses an equivalent corrected function |
| Output | Catch |
| Catch allocation | Split among fisheries using their average catch proportions over the last five years (`split.is`) |

## Harvest control rule parameters

| Parameter | Value |
|---|---:|
| Minimum catch, `min` | 270 |
| Limit metric, `lim` | 0.1 |
| Trigger metric | 2.12 |
| Target catch | 2000 |
| Initial catch for constraints | 1385 |
| Maximum annual decrease multiplier, `dlow` | 0.85 |
| Maximum annual increase multiplier, `dupp` | 1.20 |

The trigger is the value obtained during tuning and reported in the July
presentation. Before formal use it should be copied from the approved CMP
record, not recalculated by repeating the tuning exercise.

## Rule

Let $I_t$ be the annual mean of the standardized index information calculated
by the specified method. Before applying the annual change limit, the catch
recommendation $C_t^*$ is

$$
C_t^* =
\begin{cases}
270, & I_t \le 0.1,\\
270 + (I_t-0.1)\dfrac{2000-270}{2.12-0.1}, & 0.1 < I_t < 2.12,\\
2000, & I_t \ge 2.12.
\end{cases}
$$

The annual change rule then prevents the recommendation from falling below 85%
or rising above 120% of the previous recommendation. For the first application
year, the current calculation uses 1385 as the previous value.

## Evidence status

The July presentation identifies MP29 as the optimal hockeystick minimum/limit
run and reports less year-to-year catch variation (IACC) than MP32, with
slightly higher short-term catches. The `jmMSE` robustness analysis carries
MP29 and MP32 forward across alternative operating models. Final adoption still
requires complete, repeatable robustness results and approved annual data rules.

Primary sources: [`model_tune.R`](https://github.com/SPRFMO/jmMSE/blob/main/model_tune.R),
[`model_robustness.R`](https://github.com/SPRFMO/jmMSE/blob/main/model_robustness.R),
and the [July presentation source](https://github.com/SPRFMO/jmMSE/blob/main/doc/presentation/presentation_20260725.Rmd).

## Annual application requirements still to resolve

- authoritative provider and annual cutoff for each index;
- precise index standardization performed by `cpues.ind` and the software version used;
- treatment of missing or revised indices;
- catch units and rounding;
- approved catch-allocation update rule; and
- exceptional-circumstances review and decision process.
